Runs autonomously and applies its output without human review.
Override {pct}% is above the 30% gate — consider Gated.

A human reviews every output before it is applied.

agent_orchestrator.evalAssertions.confir: Delete this assertion? Future runs stop being scored against it.
agent_orchestrator.evalAssertions.form.j: Free identifier for this judge assertion (unique per scope).
agent_orchestrator.evalAssertions.form.k: Optional identifier, unique per agent. Leave empty to use the scorer name. Set it to add more than one assertion using the same scorer.
agent_orchestrator.evalAssertions.form.r: Used by LLM-judge assertions. The judge scores the run output against this rubric.
agent_orchestrator.evalAssertions.form.s: Picks the built-in check that grades each run. The fields below change to match it.
Only deterministic scorers can gate. An LLM judge is always a warning.
agent_orchestrator.evalAssertions.list.e: Create an assertion to start grading agent runs.
agent_orchestrator.evalAssertions.list.s: Configure deterministic and LLM-judge assertions applied to agent runs.
agent_orchestrator.evalAssertions.scorer: Run cost in minor currency units.
Use this to assert that the agent escalated to a human instead of auto-approving.
Dot path, e.g. result.items[0].id. Leave empty to use the whole output.
Expected value from the eval case, or a fixed value below. Only a fixed value works on production runs, which have no eval case.
Exact tool name. Suggestions come from the tools registered in this workspace.
Used when comparing against a fixed value.
Dot paths excluded from the comparison.
For "is one of", separate options with commas.
Make the judge state its reasoning before committing to a verdict. Recommended.
The judge scores the run output against this rubric.
Pass when the reply answers the customer’s question completely, stays on topic, and invents no facts that are not in the ticket. Fail when it is vague, off-topic, or contradicts the ticket.
Repeat the judgement N times and take a majority vote. Use an odd number; more samples measure variance but cost more.
Normalized judge score, 0 to 1, at or above which the assertion passes.
Every key listed must be present on the output object.
Number of tool calls in the run.
JSON object. Partial mode checks only the fields listed here.
Fail if the run made more tool calls than this — useful for catching runaway loops.
Fail if the run made fewer tool calls than this.
Count only tools matching this glob. Leave empty to count every tool call in the run.
Tool names or glob patterns, in the order expected. Suggestions come from the registered tools.
Fail if the tool was called more times than this. Leave empty for no upper limit.
Fail if the tool was called fewer times than this. 1 means it must be called at least once.

agent_orchestrator.tasks.api.authNote: Authenticate with an API key (x-api-key header) whose role grants agent_orchestrator.tasks.run; runs are attributed as api_key:<id>.

agent_orchestrator.tasks.api.responseNot: Responds 202 with { taskRunId, status: "running" } — observe completion in the run history or via task_run.* events.
